questions.question: Which of the following statements is a correct application of the Well Ordering Principle using Mathematical Induction?
options: A) If a set has 5 elements, then it has an element that is greater than or equal to all other elements
B) If a set has 3 elements, then it has an element that is greater than or equal to all other elements
C) If a set S has a smallest element, then S has a least element
D) If a set has 2 elements, then it has an element that is greater than or equal to all other elements
questions.explanation: The Well Ordering Principle states that every non-empty set of positive integers has a smallest element. This can be proven using Mathematical Induction.

questions.question: What is the statement to be proven in the Well Ordering Principle using Mathematical Induction?
options: A) All positive integers are equal
B) There exists an element in every set of positive integers
C) Every set of positive integers has a smallest element
D) Every set of positive integers has a greatest element
questions.explanation: The statement to be proven in the Well Ordering Principle using Mathematical Induction is 'Every set of positive integers has a smallest element'.

questions.question: Which of the following is a property of the Well Ordering Principle using Mathematical Induction?
options: A) It only works for finite sets
B) It only works for infinite sets
C) It only works for sets with distinct elements
D) It only works for sets with the same elements
questions.explanation: The Well Ordering Principle using Mathematical Induction only works for finite sets.

questions.question: What is the purpose of the Base Case in the Well Ordering Principle using Mathematical Induction?
options: A) To prove that every set of positive integers has an element greater than or equal to all other elements
B) To prove that every set of positive integers has a smallest element
C) To prove that every set of positive integers has a greatest element
D) To establish the inductive hypothesis
questions.explanation: The Base Case in the Well Ordering Principle using Mathematical Induction is used to establish the inductive hypothesis, which states that the statement is true for the smallest element in the set.

questions.question: Which of the following is a key step in the Well Ordering Principle using Mathematical Induction?
options: A) To prove that the statement is true for all positive integers
B) To prove that the statement is true for the smallest element in the set
C) To prove that the statement is true for all elements greater than the smallest element
D) To prove that the statement is true for all positive integers greater than 1
questions.explanation: A key step in the Well Ordering Principle using Mathematical Induction is to prove that the statement is true for the smallest element in the set.
